title: Ear pain follow-up

examination.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
examination.rationale: The exact adult hearing section supports interval pain, discharge, hearing, tinnitus, vertigo, asymmetry, relevant negatives, ENT examination, and clinician concern without determining the source of otalgia.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.3.2 — additional symptoms, ear findings, and referral

investigations.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
investigations.rationale: The exact audiology section supports concerns, reviewed hearing information, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending medicine, imaging, or referral.
investigations.evidence_extract: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.8.1 — audiology assessment, management, and support

follow_up.text: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.
follow_up.rationale: The exact acute-otitis-externa symptom section was reviewed for canal pain and modifying factors, but no additional broad legacy text was mapped because follow-up otalgia can have non-canal causes.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

source.title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements

exact_section.heading: Fact sheet — AOE symptoms and modifying factors

follow_up.text: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.
follow_up.rationale: The exact paediatric acute-otitis-media section was reviewed for age-limited reassessment context, but no adult or cause-agnostic text was mapped.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

source_limitations: Age, laterality, pain character, fever, discharge, hearing, vertigo, jaw or dental features, examination, diagnosis, treatment, response, investigation, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
